digest.component_or_area: Jira reconciler outbound identity resolution (flow layer)
propositions: Add a provider-neutral identity seam (resolve_mapping, jira_account_id, identity_email) keyed on opaque external_id (never email) that resolves local assignee/reporter strings to Jira accountIds and never raises.

All identity/mapping resolution must live in the reconciler flow layer (outbound_differ.py, dispatch_one.py); acli.py/acli_rest.py receive only a bool flag and resolved accountId strings to preserve the engine-vs-core layering and avoid circular imports.

Resolve outbound assignee via an identity-mapping fast-path in the differ, extending _assignee_resolver to a 3-tuple with an is_account_id sentinel carried inside the changed dict so acli submits the accountId directly and skips validate_assignee_exists.

When no identity mapping exists, best-effort resolve the assignee's email via a transient /user/search exact-email (case-insensitive) lookup, treating zero or multiple matches as no result and degrading to the string-match/unassigned path without failing.

Apply reporter through a dedicated REST set_reporter sub-call (via _direct_rest_put_raw) resolved by accountId before the allowlist filter, dropping the reporter and recording an outbound-reporter-unresolved soft-fail when it maps to no identity.

Isolate reporter failures to HTTP 4xx around the set_reporter call, recording an outbound-reporter-not-permitted alert and continuing the sync so other fields still apply and the run does not hard-fail.
